established.description: Established patient office visit; may not require presence of a physician or other qualified health care professional
Established patient office visit requiring straightforward MDM or 10-19 minutes of total time
Established patient office visit requiring low complexity MDM or 20-29 minutes of total time
Established patient office visit requiring moderate complexity MDM or 30-39 minutes of total time
Established patient office visit requiring high complexity MDM or 40-54 minutes of total time

new.description: New patient office visit requiring straightforward MDM or 15-29 minutes of total time
New patient office visit requiring low complexity MDM or 30-44 minutes of total time
New patient office visit requiring moderate complexity MDM or 45-59 minutes of total time
New patient office visit requiring high complexity MDM or 60-74 minutes of total time

straightforward.data: Minimal or none: review of prior external notes or each unique test ordered
straightforward.risk: Minimal risk of morbidity from additional diagnostic testing or treatment

low.problems: Low: 2 or more self-limited or minor problems; 1 acute, uncomplicated illness or injury; 1 stable chronic illness
low.data: Limited: order and/or review of each unique test; review of prior external notes with each unique source; independent historian(s)
low.risk: Low risk of morbidity from additional diagnostic testing or treatment. Examples: OTC drugs, minor surgery with no identified risk factors, physical/occupational therapy

moderate.problems: Moderate: 1 or more chronic illnesses with mild exacerbation, progression, or side effects of treatment; 2 or more stable chronic illnesses; 1 undiagnosed new problem with uncertain prognosis; 1 acute illness with systemic symptoms; 1 acute complicated injury
moderate.data: Moderate: independent interpretation of tests; discussion of management or test with external physician; or 3 of the following unique sources: review/order tests, review prior external notes, review external records, independent historian
moderate.risk: Moderate risk of morbidity from additional diagnostic testing or treatment. Examples: prescription drug management, decision regarding minor surgery with identified risk factors, decision regarding elective major surgery without identified risk factors, diagnosis or treatment significantly limited by social determinants of health

high.problems: High: 1 or more chronic illnesses with severe exacerbation, progression, or side effects of treatment; 1 acute or chronic illness or injury that poses a threat to life or bodily function
high.data: Extensive: independent interpretation of test performed by another physician and discussion of management or test with external physician or independent historian (or 2 of 3)
high.risk: High risk of morbidity from additional diagnostic testing or treatment. Examples: drug therapy requiring intensive monitoring for toxicity, decision regarding emergency major surgery, decision regarding hospitalization, decision not to resuscitate or to de-escalate care due to poor prognosis

99211: May be reported for a nurse visit or other clinical staff encounter
Physician supervision may be required depending on state law and payer policy
Document the service provided and medical necessity
99212: Document at least 1 self-limited or minor problem
Include assessment and plan for each problem addressed
99213: Document 2+ self-limited problems OR 1 acute uncomplicated illness OR 1 stable chronic illness
Include limited data review: order/review of tests, review of external records, or use of independent historian
Clearly document assessment and plan for each problem
99214: Document 1+ chronic illness with exacerbation OR 2+ stable chronic illnesses OR 1 undiagnosed new problem with uncertain prognosis
Include moderate data review: independent interpretation of tests OR discussion with external physician OR 3+ data categories
Document moderate risk: prescription drug management, or decision regarding minor surgery with risk factors
Link each problem to a specific assessment and plan
99215: Document a chronic illness with severe exacerbation OR an acute/chronic condition posing threat to life or bodily function
Include extensive data review: independent interpretation AND discussion with external physician or independent historian
Document high risk: intensive drug monitoring, emergency surgery decision, or hospitalization decision
Thorough documentation of complexity is essential to support this level
99202: Same MDM as 99212 but for a new patient
Document complete history including past medical, family, and social history
Include review of systems relevant to the presenting problem
99203: Same MDM as 99213 but for a new patient
More comprehensive history is expected for new patients
Document chief complaint, HPI, and relevant ROS
99204: Same MDM as 99214 but for a new patient
Comprehensive history and examination documentation expected
Detail the complexity of problems and data reviewed
99205: Same MDM as 99215 but for a new patient
Highest level of MDM complexity required
Extensive documentation of all elements is critical
Consider whether the patient's condition truly poses a threat to life or bodily function
